creative_brief: Clean, minimal design with a single hero image showing a named farm (e.g., Bundarra or Rachel's property) alongside the product it supplies. Use a muted earth-tone palette. Let white space carry the pacing — the copy does the heavy lifting.
subject: Fresh food shouldn't need a 2,400km head start
preheader: Your beef travels further than most Australians holiday. There's a shorter way.
body:
Hi {first_name},

Here's something most people never question: if your steak is "fresh," why has it been in cold storage for up to three weeks?

Supermarket beef averages 2,400km from paddock to shelf. Then 14–21 days in cold storage before it hits the display case. By the time you cook it, the animal that was raised who-knows-how has travelled further than your last road trip.

We assumed that was normal. It's not. It's just cheap logistics.

Fresh should mean days old, not weeks old. It should mean you can name the farm. And the animal should have lived on open pasture — no hormones, no antibiotics, no feedlot finishing.

That's exactly what we built FarmThru to do.

Our beef is 100% grass-fed from regenerative farms. Our salmon comes wild-caught from Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast of NSW. Bundarra Farm supplies our pork belly and salami. Farmer Brown does our eggs. Paris Creek Farms in Meadows, SA, handles our carbon-neutral bio-dynamic dairy.

These aren't suppliers. They're partners we visit, whose practices we verify, whose names we print on the label.

Rachel Ward — who runs a regenerative farm in Kempsey, NSW — put it simply: "People deserve to know where their food comes from. Not a brand story. The actual place."

So how does it work? You order online. You collect from our Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday. No commitment, no subscription lock-in — just order when you want.

The food is days old when you pick it up. Straight from their farm to our hub to your kitchen.

That's it. No 2,400km journey. No mystery cold chain. Just food that tastes the way it's supposed to because nothing was done to make it worse.

Come see what real fresh looks like.

[Explore what's on the hub →]

Rachel & the FarmThru team

P.S. Pick up your first order this week. You'll taste the difference before you finish cooking.
